bills.title: RELATING TO TRANSPORTATION.
bills.last_action: Introduced.
bills.raw_subject:
Conforms ROH provisions to House Bill 2021, HD2, SD2, CD1, Relating to Transportation. Adds definitions for “Electric Bicycle,” “Class 1 Electric Bicycle,” Class 2 Electric Bicycle,” Class 3 Electric Bicycle, “Electric Micro-mobility Device, “High-speed Electric Device,” and “Electric Motorcycle.” Substitutes the term “electric bicycle” for the terms “bicycle with motor,” “motorized bicycles,” and similar terms. Substitutes the term “motor-driven cycle” for the term “motor scooter.” Regulates riding electric bicycles on sidewalks under certain circumstances. Prohibits on bikeways: (1) driving a vehicle, other than a bicycle, electric foot scooter, or electric micro-mobility device, with specified
exceptions; and (2) operating mopeds or electric motorcycles. Prohibits a person under the age of sixteen from operating a class 2 or class 3 electric bicycle without direct supervision. Repeals as preempted: (1) certain safe driving requirements for the operation of electric bicycles on public streets, paths, or tra

bills.title: RELATING TO CULTURAL SITES IN PUBLIC PARKS.
bills.last_action: Postponed to a date and time to be determined by the Committee Chair.
bills.raw_subject: Requires the DPR Director to establish an Important Cultural Resource Area program to allow for improved stewardship of important cultural resources in City parks. Establishes the process for including City parks in the program and requires a stewardship plan for included parks. Provides the DPR Director with rulemaking authority. Imposes fines for violations.

bills.title: RELATING TO PUBLIC PARKS, RECREATIONAL FACILITIES, AND BOTANICAL GARDENS.
bills.last_action:
See: Proposed draft version OCS2026-0182/3/30/2026 2:35 PM
Amended to CD1 and postponed to a date and time to be determined by the Committee Chair.
bills.raw_subject: Repeals certain specific fees and charges for the use of public parks and recreational facilities. Authorizes the DPR Director to establish fees and charges by rule for the commercial and noncommercial use of parks; recreational facilities; and botanical gardens under the jurisdiction of the DPR and requires that the fees and charges collected (except those derived from the use of the Hanauma Bay Nature Preserve) be deposited into a fund designated by the DPR Director. Provides the DPR Director with rulemaking authority. Establishes reporting requirements. Takes effect 450 days after approval; provided that fees established by ROH articles and sections to be repealed will remain in effect until the DPR Director adopts rules.

bills.title: RELATING TO FUNDS.
bills.last_action:
See: Proposed draft version OCS2026-0188/3/30/2026 12:02 PM

bills.raw_subject: Establishes a special fund to be called the Parks Maintenance and Recreation Services Revolving Fund for the deposit of all revenues and proceeds generated by the DPR relating to the use of lands dedicated for park use or for park maintenance and recreation services, or otherwise earmarked for the restoration, operation, or maintenance of beaches and parks under the jurisdiction of the DPR, to be expended for DPR operations and parks maintenance and recreational facilities under the jurisdiction of the DPR. Establishes a special fund to be called the Botanical Gardens and Urban Forestry Revolving Fund for the deposit of all revenues and proceeds relating to the use of lands dedicated for botanical gardens, community gardens, and urban forestry programs and operations generated by the DPR, or otherwise earmarked for the restoration, operation, or maintenance of parks, beaches, gardens, and street trees under the jurisdiction of the DPR, to be expended for the operations of the DPR’s Division of Urban Forestry

bills.title: RELATING TO CLEAN WATER AND NATURAL LANDS FUND.
bills.last_action: Passed first reading.
bills.raw_subject: Establishes specific processes and procedures for evaluating proposals for expenditures from the Clean Water and Natural Lands Fund. Requires the HLM to perform preliminary due diligence on each proposal for the use of funds in the Clean Water and Natural Lands Fund. Requires a status of the land transaction report six months after Council approval! of a qualified proposal, and every six months thereafteruntil the land transaction closes.

bills.title: RELATING TO THE REAL PROPERTY TAX EXEMPTION FOR LOW-INCOME RENTAL HOUSING.
bills.last_action: Passed first reading.
bills.raw_subject: Revises the real property tax exemption for low-income rental housing projects to apply to the assessed value of only the portion of the project's building and land attributed to the exempt floor area for dwelling units reserved for low-income households during a regulated period of at least 15 years.

bills.title: RELATING TO AFFORDABLE HOUSING INCENTIVES.
bills.last_action: Second reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Requires the HLM to submit semi-annual reports detailing all types of incentives, along with the total amount and value of each incentive, received by every affordable housing project that has received one or more incentives from the City.

bills.title: RELATING TO AFFORDABLE HOUSING REQUIREMENTS.
bills.last_action: Postponed.
bills.raw_subject: Repeals affordable housing requirement options for a developer of an IPD-T project, a PD-T project, or a TOD special district project seeking bonus height or density, or both, that required for-sale affordable housing units to remain affordable for a minimum of 30 years. Repeals the marketing period requirements for for-rental and for-sale affordable dwelling units created in compliance with ROH Chapter 29.

CR-141(26)

bills.title: RELATING TO THE REGULATION OF DOGS.
bills.last_action: Passed first reading.
bills.raw_subject: Requires the CSD Director or the head of the City agency designated by the Mayor to require the animal control contractor to enforce the provisions contained in ROH Chapter 12, Article 4. Increases existing criminal fines and establishes new civil fines.

bills.raw_subject: Amends the repeal date of ROH Chapter 32, relating to affordable rental housing, to July 1, 2027. Effective July 1, 2027, repeals related provisions to reflect the proposed July 1, 2027 repeal date for ROH Chapter 32.

bills.title: RELATING TO PARKING.
bills.last_action: Passed first reading.
bills.raw_subject: Modifies ROH Title IV, Chapter 15, Article 2, Article 22, Article 23, and Article 28 relating to traffic and vehicles, for the regulation of on-street and off-street public parking. Defines the” Parking Meter Zone” and authorizes the addition or subtraction of paid public parking within it. Adds flexibility to the City’s parking facilities operations model (metered v. concession) and makes conforming amendments to article sections. Updates hourly on-street and off-street parking rate maximums and minimums. Establishes variable “tiered” parking rates for extended stays at metered parking. Authorizes the incremental adjustment to minimum and secondary hourly parking rates on street segments and in off-street parking areas according to monitored parking occupancy. Updates methods of payment for all metered parking. Updates parking rate change communication requirements and allows for discounts to low-income residents. Allows for outsourcing parking violation enforcement including the lawful issuance of tickets b

bills.raw_subject: Requires the BFS Director, no later than January 1, 2027, to develop, maintain, and make publicly available a searchable, online database of City revenues and expenditures that is free, open to the public, updated at least monthly, and accessible through an open checkbook-style, web-based system. Requires the database to include specified expenditure information and protect confidential information.

bills.title: RELATING TO SHARED STREETS.
bills.last_action: Second reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Establishes a Shared Streets Program to provide a safe, accessible, and efficient space for all users of designated shared street areas, with priority given to pedestrians and other non-motorized users. To establish a designated shared street area, requires the affirmative recommendation from the DTS Director, the opportunity for public participation, and Council approval by resolution.

bills.title: RELATING TO AFFORDABLE RENTAL HOUSING.
bills.last_action: Passed first reading.
bills.raw_subject: Adds business zoning district as a zoning district in which affordable rental housing projects are a permitted use. Amends affordable rental housing development standards and loading requirements. Deletes figures illustrating possible configurations of maximum building area and required yards. Amends formula for determining the maximum number of affordable rental housing units for each zoning lot. Repeals ROH Section 32-2.9, relating to the maximum size of units. Deletes provisions relating to allowable building areas. Amends provisions relating to building height maximum standards. Deletes Table 32-A relating to building heights and areas. Adds standards for corner lots. Adds requirement that all structures on a lot developed under ROH Chapter 32 must adhere to the development standards therein. Amends effective date section of Ordinance 19-8, as amended by Ordinance 23-12. Amends effective date section of Ordinance 18-1, as amended by Ordinance 25-25.

bills.title: RELATING TO TRANSPORTATION.
bills.last_action:
See: Proposed draft version (OCS2026-0117/2/25/2026 4:41 PM)
Amended to CD1 and postponed to a date and time to be determined by the Committee Chair.
bills.raw_subject: Amends various provisions relating to traffic and vehicles. Requires the DTS Directorto apply standards established in the MUTCD when locating, selecting, installing, and maintaining City traffic control devices. Requires the DTS Director to maintain certain traffic schedules of traffic control devices on the DTS website. Permits the use of skateboards, roller skates, toy vehicles, and similar devices within bicycle lanes located in the Waikiki Special District. Repeals ROH § 15-3.3, which authorizes the BFS Director to designate authorized tow vehicles. Changes the minimum property damage threshold for filing a subsequent report of a motor vehicle accident from $300 to $_. Updates the type of warning lights used at mid-block crosswalks from “amber colored lamps” to “flashing pedestrian beacons.” Authorizes vehicles to traverse, encroach, or stand in a City transit bus lane when making a right-hand turn. Requires pavement markings and signage for bicycle lanes delineated on City highways to conform to the

bills.last_action: Passed first reading.
bills.raw_subject: Establishes a valuation cap (either 2 percent of the preceding tax year’s assessed value of the real property or the rate of inflation per year, whichever is lower) for real property owned and occupied by a taxpayer 65 years or age or older. Specifies certain exceptions to the valuation cap. Effective for tax years beginning July 1, 2027.

bills.title: RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAXATION.
bills.last_action: Passed first reading.
bills.raw_subject: Amends the definition of income in ROH § 8-13.1 to clarify that only the titleholder’s income (and not the income of a non-titleholder who is included on a joint income tax return) will be considered when calculating income eligibility for the County Tax Credit.

bills.raw_subject: Beginning December 31, 2025, and every three months thereafter, requires the BFS Director to compile and transmit to the Council a consolidated report of all individual transfers of funds between activities and all individual transfers of funds between characters of expenditure within the same activity occurring within each quarter. Requires that the report include specified information for the preceding quarter and for each of the preceding 11 quarterly periods. Beginning July 1, 2026, and every year thereafter, requires the BFS Director to compile and transmit a consolidated report of all fiscal year-end total cumulative amounts of funds transferred. Requires that the consolidated report include specified information for the preceding fiscal year.

bills.title:
Recommending the following:

1.	ADOPTION of Resolution 25-153, to approve for inclusion in the 2026 Hawai‘i State Association of Counties Legislative Package a proposed State bill to:  1) create a new Hawai‘i Revised Statutes chapter for Psychology Interjurisdictional Compact, an interstate compact to facilitate the practice of telepsychology and the temporary in-person practice of psychology across state boundaries; and 2) authorize the state-to-state practice of qualifying psychologists and help reduce regulatory barriers by allowing more psychologists to practice in the state, thus improving access to essential mental health care; and

2.	ADOPTION of Resolution 25-154, to approve for inclusion in the 2026 Maui County Council Legislative Package a proposed State bill to:  1) create a new Hawai‘i Revised Statutes chapter for Psychology Interjurisdictional Compact, an interstate compact to facilitate the practice of telepsychology and the temporary in-person practice of psychology across state boundaries; and 2) authorize the state-to-state practice of qualifying psychologists and help reduce regulatory barriers by allowing more psychologists to practice in the state, thus improving access to essential mental health care.

bills.title: RELATING TO DUMPSTERS.
bills.last_action: Public hearing notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Requires that dumpsters, whether publicly- or privately-owned, placed on a public street or right-of-way for the collection of refuse or waste be equipped with two red reflectors on each side, excluding the top and bottom of the dumpster, one at or near the front and one at or near the rear of each side. Prohibits the placement in a public street or right-of-way of a dumpster that does not comply with the reflector requirement, a violation of which is punishable by a civil fine not exceeding $500 for each violation.

bills.title: RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAX EXEMPTIONS.
bills.last_action: Passed first reading.
bills.raw_subject:
Amends the real property tax exemption for low-income rental housing to require property owners to allocate the benefits of the tax exemption exclusively to a rental assistance program for persons who are low-income and reside in the housing project
or to the maintenance and preservation ofthe housing project. Takes effect on July 1 2027.

bills.title: RELATING TO SEWER SERVICE CHARGES.
bills.last_action: Final reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Repeals the requirement that industrial users who discharge or cause to be discharged certain wastewaters into the public sewers pay a surcharge in accordance with ROH Section 43-6.6 ("Nonresidential strength surcharges"), consistent with the repeal of that section by Ordinance 25-27 (Bill 60 (2024), CD2, approved on June 24, 2025).

bills.title: RELATING TO SPECIAL MANAGEMENT AREAS.
bills.last_action: Passed first reading.
bills.raw_subject: Requires an applicant for a special management area minor permit to present the subject project to the neighborhood board of the district where the project is located or, if no such neighborhood board exists, then an appropriate community association. Requires the applicant provide written notice of the presentation to owners of all properties adjoining the proposed development. Provides that the presentation requirement will be deemed satisfied if, upon written request of the applicant to present, the neighborhood board or community association fails to provide the applicant with an opportunity to present within 60 days of a written request, or the neighborhood board or community association provides the applicant with written notice that it has no objection to the proposed development.

bills.title: RELATING TO MULTI-FAMILY RENTAL HOUSING PROGRAM FEES.
bills.last_action: Final reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Creates a Multi-Family Rental Housing Program account within the Housing Development Special Fund, moneys from which must be expended solely for the purpose of operating the Multi-Family Rental Housing Program. Creates fees for the Multi-Family Rental Housing Program that must be deposited into the Multi-Family Rental Housing Program account. Specifies that the Director of Housing and Land Management, instead of the Director of Community Services, is authorized to adopt rules for the Multi Family Rental Housing Program.

bills.title: RELATING TO PUBLIC TRANSIT.
bills.last_action: Final reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Amends certain provisions of ROH Chapter 15B related to the fare structure for Oahu's Multimodal Municipal Transportation System. Repeals and replaces the Fare Structure Table to, among other things, increase prices charged for certain passenger categories; incentivize the use of the HOLO card over cash fares; and establish a combined reduced fare category for the following passengers: Senior Citizens, Persons with a Disability, Paratransit Eligibility ID Holders, Medicare cardholders, and Individuals with Extremely Low Income (provided that the reduced fare for the monthly pass and the annual pass are only eligible to passengers who are Hawaiʻi residents). Increases the price of the special reduced term seven-day pass. Authorizes the DTS to establish a special event single ride fare for certain special events. Authorizes the DTS to establish a special single ride fare for transportation to and from the Daniel K. lnouye International Airport. Amends the fare amounts for individuals with extremely low incomes

bills.last_action: Public hearing notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Requires the construction of new affordable rental housing projects under ROH Chapter 32 to contain one parking stall for every two units within each project. Also requires a resident manager unit and employment of a resident manager. Resident manager units may not be counted towards the affordable rental unit requirement. Requires all project developers to make a presentation to the relevant neighborhood board.

Amends Fort Street Mall Special Improvement District No. 2, established by Ordinance 02-48, as amended by Ordinance 07-44, relating to the Fort Street Mall Special Improvement District No. 2., and supersedes any conflicting provisions. Renames the
Fort Street Mall Special Improvement District No. 2 to the Downtown Honolulu Business Improvement District No. 2. Replaces the Fort Street Mall Special Improvement District Plan with the Downtown Honolulu Business Improvement District Plan. Revises the District's boundaries and the list of parcels of land to be assessed. Makes revisions to the District's supplemental services and improvements to be provided or financed and the rate or method of apportionment of the existing special assessment.

bills.title: RELATING TO AGREEMENTS.
bills.last_action: Postponed to a date and time to be determined by the Committee Chair.
bills.raw_subject: Requires all City departments to submit a report to the Council detailing an intergovernmental agreement or a private grant agreement made with the City, at least 15 days before being entered into or used. Further requires certain agreements that involve city expenditure below of $30 million or less, or otherwise utilize City resources, to be reported to the Council in order to become effective. Provides that a Councilmember may file written objection to these agreements within 15 days after the report is submitted to the Council; to become effective, these agreements must be approved by the Council via resolution.

bills.title: RELATING TO THE CITY TRANSIT SYSTEM.
bills.last_action: CC-181(25) - Bill re-referred from Planning, Infrastructure & Transportation to Infrastructure, Transportation & Technology Committee.
bills.raw_subject: Amends sections of Chapter 1 5B, Public Transit, Revised Ordinances of Honolulu 2021, to promote public safety, system security, service quality, and multimodal transit options on the City’s transit system. Emphasizes the core purpose of the city’s multimodal municipal transportation system, that passenger protection and safety includes keeping passengers safe from illness in a COVID-19 world, and the protection and preservation of transit vehicles and facilities representing billions of dollars of capital investment. Adopts various definitions related to weapons from State law and adds a new definition of a non-firearm weapon, to expand and clarify the types of instruments, tools, or objects that may be deemed a weapon under certain circumstances. Adds new passenger conduct to prohibited activities including the carrying, possessing, threatening, or using of non-firearm weapons as defined in the ordinance, and entering or utilizing a city transit vehicle or facility for other than transportation-related purp

bills.title: RELATING TO FISCAL STABILITY.
bills.last_action: Failed to pass third reading.
bills.raw_subject:
Temporarily allows the City Administration to expend moneys from the Reserve for Fiscal Stability Fund upon the Mayor's written notice to the Council and filing with the City Clerk a notice that includes the Mayor's determination that there are insufficient moneys to provide services necessary to protect the health, safety, or welfare of City residents and that expenditures from the Reserve for Fiscal Stability Fund are necessary to provide these services, and other specified information. Council approval by resolution is required for expenditures exceeding $500,000. Authority to expend funds that have not been approved by Council resolution expires 90 calendar days after the date the Mayor’s written notice is filed with the City Clerk unless extended by Council resolution.
Requires the expenditures to be discussed at a public meeting of the Council or a Council committee and the Mayor or the Mayor's designee to present specified information regarding the expenditures at the meeting.

bills.raw_subject: Requires that if one enforcement notice is issued by the DPP Director to more than one person responsible for a violation, those persons are responsible for compliance and are jointly and severally liable for any fines imposed in a notice of order. Requires that if more than one enforcement notice is issued by the DPP Director to more than one person responsible for a violation, each person is responsible for compliance and any fines imposed by a notice of order. Provides, among other things, that service is effective as to all persons named in an enforcement notice issued by the DPP Director upon the completion of the last required publication of the enforcement notice in a daily or weekly publication that is in general circulation within the city; and by the physical posting of a copy of an enforcement notice in a prominent location on the property that is the subject of the enforcement notice. bills.raw_subject: Council of the County of Maui

bills.title:
"A BILL FOR AN ORDINANCE AMENDING THE FISCAL YEAR 2025 BUDGET FOR THE COUNTY OF MAUI, ESTIMATED REVENUES; CAPITAL IMPROVEMENT PROJECTS, DEPARTMENT OF WATER SUPPLY; APPENDIX A, PART II, GENERAL EXCISE TAX FUND; AND APPENDIX C, DEPARTMENT OF MANAGEMENT AND DEPARTMENT OF WATER SUPPLY"

The purpose of this bill is to amend the Fiscal Year 2025 Budget to reflect the transfer of $25,000,000 from the General Excise Tax Fund to the Water Fund-Restricted for the following projects:  $10,000,000 for Water Supply Infrastructure Repairs and Replacements in wildfire-impacted areas (GET Fund), $10,000,000 for Integration of Public and Private Water Systems (GET Fund), and $5,000,000 for Kamole Water Treatment Plant Filter Replacement (GET Fund).
bills.raw_subject: Council of the County of Maui

bills.title:
"A BILL FOR AN ORDINANCE AMENDING CHAPTER 3.100, MAUI COUNTY CODE, RELATING TO GENERAL EXCISE AND USE TAX SURCHARGE"

The purpose of this bill is amend Chapter 3.100, Maui County Code, to: a) Clarify that 80 percent of all funds derived from the County’s General Excise and Use Tax Surcharge will be deposited into the General Excise Tax Fund and 20 percent will be deposited into the General Excise Tax Fund - Department of Hawaiian Home Lands; and b) Provide for funding for water-related projects from either fund to be transferred to the Department of Water Supply for the duration of the project and for remaining funds to be re-deposited in the appropriate fund at the conclusion of the project.

bills.title: RELATING TO ELECTRONIC AMUSEMENT DEVICES.
bills.last_action: Final reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Creates a regulatory system of permits for the display and use of electronic amusement devices. Designates the Chief of Police of the HPD, or the chief's designee, as the official responsible for the issuance of the electronic amusement device permits. Provides the Chief of Police of the HPD with rulemaking authority. Establishes penalties for non-compliance.

bills.title: RELATING TO GAMBLING.
bills.last_action: Final reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Requires cooperation between the Director of Planning and Permitting and Honolulu Chief of Police to ensure code enforcement of gambling-related public nuisances. Provides the DPP and the HPD with rulemaking authority.

bills.title: RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY.
bills.last_action: Final reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Allows an owner or landlord of private real property to request the designation of the HPD as the owner’s or landlord’s agent to inspect the property in accordance with the lease, rental agreement, or HRS Chapter 521 (the Residential Landlord-Tenant Code), for a term not to exceed 12 months, subject to approval by the Chief of Police. Requires that an inspecting police officer provide advance notice of the inspection pursuant to the lease, rental agreement, or the Residential Landlord-Tenant Code; inspect the property during reasonable hours, to include 6:00 a.m. to 10:00 p.m.; and show the consent for the inspection to any person on the premises during the inspection upon their request. Allows the inspecting police officer to request a person not authorized to be present on the property to leave and not return pursuant to HRS Section 708-814, relating to criminal trespass in the second degree; document any violations of the lease in a report; and provide a copy of the report to the owner, landlord, mortgagor

bills.title: Recommending REFERRAL of bills to the Council Chair for the term beginning January 2, 2025, relating to:  (1) amending Subsection 2.96.060(B), Maui County Code, to amend the residential workforce housing deed restrictions to increase the time period for all restrictions; (2) amending Chapter 3.35, Maui County Code, to provide a stable and continuing source of funding to be used for planning, administering, and constructing affordable and workforce housing projects and to provide a policy for the use of the funding; (3) establishing an ordinance that restarts the deed-restricted period when a residential workforce housing unit is sold during the deed-restricted period; (4) amending the eligibility criteria for rental residential workforce housing units to expand eligibility to individuals or families displaced by an emergency under Chapter 127A, Hawai‘i Revised Statutes or who are income and asset qualified but held a disqualifying interest in real property within the last three years; (5) a Change the Zoning, with conditions, of properties related to Lanai Project District 2 (Koele), at Lānaʻi City, Lānaʻi, Hawaii, and an amendment to Chapter 19.71, Maui County Code, relating to Lanai Project District 2 (Koele); (6) increasing the deed-restriction period for residential workforce housing ownership units and expediting the County's purchase of residential workforce housing units when offered to the County; (7) increasing the allowable density in the R-1, R-2, and R-3 Residential Zoning Districts to provide additional housing opportunities in Maui County; (8) providing lower-cost opportunities for more autonomous living areas in a dwelling unit by allowing for kitchenettes in dwelling units in the Residential and Rural Districts on Maui and Lanaʻi and requiring a dwelling unit with a kitchenette to be occupied on a long-term residential basis; and (9) addressing nonconforming structures and uses damaged, destroyed, or affected by an emergency or disaster declared by proclamation of the Governor to constitute a state of emergency under Chapter 127A, Hawai‘i Revised Statutes.

bills.title: RELATING TO WHEELIES.
bills.last_action: Final reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Prohibits the performance of wheelies on public streets and roadways by drivers of electric bicycles, mopeds, and motorcycles, except as part of a parade, tournament, or other activity permitted by the city. Defines a “wheelie” as “[a) stunt performed by a person while riding on an electric bicycle, moped, or motorcycle in which the front wheel is raised so that the vehicle is balanced for a moment or longer on its rear wheel or wheels.” Defines “electric bicycle” as “[a] two-wheeled vehicle with handle bars, fully operable pedals, and an electric motor used either to power the vehicle unassisted, or to assist with pedaling.” Defines a “moped” to include an electric bicycle.

bills.title: TO AMEND CHAPTER 43 OF THE REVISED ORDINANCES OF HONOLULU 2021, RELATING TO SEWERS, DRAINAGE, AND CESSPOOLS.
bills.last_action: Final reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Amends various sections and appendices of ROH Chapter 43 to include Accessory Dwelling Units as a sewer customer classification; update sewer service charges and wastewater system facility charges, including an automatic 3 percent annual increase beginning fiscal year 2033 for sewer service charges and fiscal year 2032 for wastewater system facility charges; add wastewater disposal charges for sewer customers; remove references to extra strength wastewater; establish a customer assistance program within the ENV to provide relief to households paying residential sewer charges; and to allow the ENV Director or the Director's authorized representative to adopt rules to implement and administer the customer assistance program. The revised charges commence on January 1, 2026 and the repeal of certain provisions in Ordinance 25-2 takes effect upon approval.

Recommending ADOPTION of Resolution 24-71, CD1, authorizing additional compensation, in the amount of $325,000, for a total compensation not to exceed $450,000, to Kobayashi Sugita & Goda, LLP, as special counsel to represent the County of Maui, Richard T. Bissen, Jr. in his official capacity, and Kate Blystone, as successor to Kathleen Aoki, in her official capacity, in Christopher Salem v. County of Maui, et. al., Case 2CCV-21-0000048.

bills.title:
Recommending the following:

1.	ADOPTION of Resolution 24-130, CD1, to approve for inclusion in the 2025 Hawai‘i State Association of Counties Legislative Package a proposed State bill to: 1) define the public trust purposes of the Commission on Water Resource Management (“Commission”) and require the Commission, in acting upon water use permit applications, to prioritize applications for public trust purposes; 2) allow the Commission to retain independent legal counsel; 3) repeal the position of Deputy to the Chairperson of the Commission and establish the position of Executive Director of the Commission; 4) amend the composition of the Commission and administratively attach it to the Department of Land and Natural Resources; 5) authorize entities to challenge an emergency order of the Commission under certain conditions; 6) establish fines for certain water use offenses; and 7) amend the Commission’s authority to declare water emergencies and issue orders to address them and declare water shortages and related notice requirements; and

2.	ADOPTION of Resolution 24-131, CD1, to approve for inclusion in the 2025 Maui County Council Legislative Package a proposed State bill to: 1) define the public trust purposes of the Commission on Water Resource Management (“Commission”) and require the Commission, in acting upon water use permit applications, to prioritize applications for public trust purposes; 2) allow the Commission to retain independent legal counsel; 3) repeal the position of Deputy to the Chairperson of the Commission and establish the position of Executive Director of the Commission; 4) amend the composition of the Commission and administratively attach it to the Department of Land and Natural Resources; 5) authorize entities to challenge an emergency order of the Commission under certain conditions; 6) establish fines for certain water use offenses; and 7) amend the Commission’s authority to declare water emergencies and issue orders to address them and declare water shortages and related notice requirements.

bills.title: RELATING TO TRAFFIC SAFETY.
bills.last_action: Final reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Authorizes the Director of Transportation Services to post a recommended safe speed on roadways near schools. Lowers the maximum speed on roadways in public parks and public school grounds from 25 to 20 miles per hour. Creates a new 20 miles per hour speed limit zone applicable to certain roadways, including roadways bordering school grounds during certain days and times.

bills.title: RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAXATION.
bills.last_action: Councilmember Nishimoto took office on January 2, 2025.
bills.raw_subject: Establishes an empty homes tax as a supplemental real property tax on dwelling units that are vacant for a certain number of days each year and provides exemptions from the empty homes tax. Requires residential property owners to annually submit a property status declaration. Provides the Department of Budget and Fiscal Services with rulemaking authority. Establishes violations and penalties, including foreclosure of real properties having delinquent empty homes taxes and penalties for at least three consecutive years after notice.

bills.title: RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAXATION.
bills.last_action: Councilmember Nishimoto took office on January 2, 2025.
bills.raw_subject: Establishes dedications of real property as aina kupuna. Provides petition requirements to qualify for the dedication. Applies to tax years beginning July 1 2026 and thereafter.

bills.title: RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAXATION.
bills.last_action: Councilmember Nishimoto took office on January 2, 2025.
bills.raw_subject: Authorizes an owner of real property classified as Residential A to dedicate their property for use as a long-term residential rental and have such property classified as Residential. Provides the BFS Director with rulemaking authority. Applies to the tax years beginning July 1, 2026 and thereafter.

bills.title: RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAXATION.
bills.last_action: Councilmember Nishimoto took office on January 2, 2025.
bills.raw_subject: Establishes a real property tax exemption for real property whose assessed valuations exceed an unspecified percentage of the previous tax year’s assessed value and for which a homeowner’s exemption has been claimed for an unspecified number of years. Allows the real property tax exemption to continue to apply if the real property is inherited in sole ownership by a single family member who is eligible for the home exemption with respect to that real property, and the real property continues to be held in sole ownership. Effective upon the enactment of certain State legislation.

bills.title: RELATING TO PARADES, STREET FESTIVALS, AND OTHER ACTIVITIES.
bills.last_action: Final reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Updates the applicable ordinances related to activities that require the exclusive use of streets, including parades, street festivals, and other endeavors. Establishes a mechanism for for-profit parades to meet public purpose requirements. With respect to activities in the Waikiki Special District, reduces the number of permits that the DTS Director may issue per year, and authorizes the Director to issue up to 12 permits for street festivals in the district, including Waikiki legacy street festivals. Provides for administrative cost recovery for multiple applications. Clarifies the Mayor’s authority to grant waivers for activities in the Waikiki Special District. Requires permit applicants to secure appropriate medical services to support the activity. Requires street festival permit applicants to implement a liquor controlled area if alcoholic beverages will be sold. Takes effect on January 1, 2025, and applies to permits issued for 2026 and thereafter.

bills.title: RELATING TO BUILDING PERMITS.
bills.last_action: Final reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: Clarifies that all documents and information required in support of a building permit application be submitted concurrently with the building permit application. Requires that the building permit application include the name, license number, and address of all contractors, subcontractors, and specialty contractors involved in the project, or identify the project as an owner-builder project, if applicable. Requires building permit applications to state the name, address, license number, and phases of work to be performed by all contractors, subcontractors, and specialty contractors engaged to perform electrical or plumbing work on the project and a signature by the responsible managing employee or authorized employee of each contractor, subcontractor, and specialty contractor so designated/stated. Removes the requirements that building permit applicants submit an affidavit that: (1) demonstrates that a majority of the owners of a property concur with the demolition of a building; and (2) affirms compliance...

bills.title: RELATING TO OVERT VIDEO MONITORING.
bills.last_action: CC-181(25) - Bill re-referred from Public Safety & Customer Services to International & Legal Affairs Committee.
bills.raw_subject: Authorizes the implementation of overt video monitoring of public activity from fixed locations in and around the City rail system for the purpose of general prevention and deterrence of criminal activity. Amends the definition of “Responsible City Official” to authorize the Director of Transportation Services to implement overt video monitoring of public activity in and around the City rail system. Requires the designation of the “Responsible City Official” in resolutions approving video monitoring pursuant to Section 2-32.3(a)(7).

bills.title: AUTHORIZING THE ISSUANCE AND SALE AND SPECIFYING CERTAIN TERMS OF WASTEWATER SYSTEM REVENUE COMMERCIAL PAPER OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF HONOLULU IN AN AGGREGATE OUTSTANDING PRINCIPAL AMOUNT NOT TO EXCEED FOUR HUNDRED MILLION DOLLARS ($400,000,000.00) OUTSTANDING AT ANY ONE TIME, FOR THE PURPOSE OF REFUNDING ALL OR ANY PART OF ANY OUTSTANDING WASTEWATER SYSTEM REVENUE INDEBTEDNESS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OR PAYING THE COST OF ANY EQUIPMENT OR OTHER IMPROVEMENT AUTHORIZED PURSUANT TO ANY CAPITAL BUDGET ORDINANCE RELATING TO THE WASTEWATER SYSTEM OF THE CITY AND COUNTY; AUTHORIZING THE DIRECTOR OF BUDGET AND FISCAL SERVICES TO DETERMINE CERTAIN TERMS OF SUCH COMMERCIAL PAPER; PROVIDING FOR THE RETIREMENT OF ANY INDEBTEDNESS TO BE REFUNDED; AND PROVIDING FOR OTHER ACTIONS RELATED TO THE ISSUANCE, SALE AND DELIVERY OF SUCH COMMERCIAL PAPER.
bills.last_action: Final reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.
bills.raw_subject: The issuance, sale and delivery of Wastewater System Revenue commercial paper from time to time for the purpose of paying costs of improvements, equipment, and other improvements, including costs of issuance and refunding, to the Wastewater System on an interim or short-term basis.

"A BILL FOR AN ORDINANCE AMENDING SECTION 3.16.020, MAUI COUNTY CODE, RELATING TO SETTLEMENT OF CLAIMS AND OTHER CIVIL LITIGATION"

The purpose of this bill is to amend language in subsections A (Workers' Compensation Claims) and B (Other Claims or Civil Litigation) of Section 3.16.020, Maui County Code, to increase Corporation Counsel's authority to settle claims, from $7,500 to $25,000, without Council approval.
